
```{r}
library(haven)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggsci)

affairs <- read_dta("./affairs.dta")
# data(affairs, package = "AER")

head(affairs)
affmodel <- glm(naffairs ~ kids + age + yrsmarr,
    family = poisson(link = "log"),
    data = affairs
)

y_obs <- affairs$naffairs
n <- length(y_obs)

mu <- fitted(affmodel)

head(mu)
dim(affairs)
length(mu)
affmodel
exp(0.25388 + 0.01003 * 0 - 0.02904 * 37 + 0.11599 * 10)
exp(0.25388 + 0.01003 * 0 - 0.02904 * 27 + 0.11599 * 4)
set.seed(123)

# simulate one replicated dataset
y_rep <- rpois(n, lambda = mu)


B <- 500 # number of simulated datasets

y_rep_all <- unlist(
    replicate(B, rpois(n, lambda = mu))
)


hist(y_obs,
    probability = TRUE,
    breaks = 30,
    col = rgb(0, 0, 1, 0.3),
    xlim = range(c(y_obs, y_rep_all)),
    main = "Empirical vs Marginal Predicted Distribution",
    xlab = "Y (number of affairs)"
)

hist(y_rep_all,
    probability = TRUE,
    breaks = 30,
    col = rgb(1, 0, 0, 0.3),
    add = TRUE
)

legend("topright",
    legend = c("Empirical Y", "Marginal predicted Y"),
    fill = c(rgb(0, 0, 1, 0.3), rgb(1, 0, 0, 0.3))
)


dens_obs <- density(y_obs)
dens_rep <- density(y_rep_all)

plot(dens_obs,
    col = "blue",
    lwd = 2,
    main = "Empirical vs Marginal Predicted Density",
    xlab = "Y"
)

lines(dens_rep,
    col = "red",
    lwd = 2
)

legend("topright",
    legend = c("Empirical", "Model-implied"),
    col = c("blue", "red"),
    lwd = 2
)

# QQ-style comparison
qqplot(y_rep_all, y_obs,
    main = "QQ: Marginal predicted vs empirical"
)
abline(0, 1, col = "red")
```


```{r}
# Load libraries
library(DHARMa)
library(MASS)

# 1. Simulate some overdispersed count data
set.seed(123)
x <- rnorm(200)
# Data is generated with Negative Binomial (overdispersed)
# but we will fit a Poisson to see the "misfit"
y <- rnbinom(200, mu = exp(1 + 0.5 * x), size = 1)

# 2. Fit a Poisson GLM (potentially misspecified)
model <- glm(y ~ x, family = poisson)

# 3. Create simulated residuals (Model-implied vs. Empirical)
# This simulates the model-implied distribution 250 times
sim_res <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = model, n = 250)

# 4. Plot the comparison
# The QQ plot shows the PIT (Probability Integral Transform) check
# The Residual vs Predicted shows if the variance matches
plot(sim_res)
```

```{r}
# Setup sample data (Gamma GLM)
set.seed(42)
x <- runif(500, 1, 10)
y <- rgamma(500, shape = 2, scale = x / 2)

# Fit the model
model <- glm(y ~ x, family = Gamma)


# 1. Empirical Density (The Real Data)
plot(density(y),
    main = "KDE vs. Model-Implied Marginal",
    lwd = 2, col = "black", ylim = c(0, 0.4)
)

# 2. Model-Implied Density (Simulation)
# We generate 100 simulated datasets from our model's predictions
y_sim <- simulate(model, nsim = 100)

dim(y_sim)
# Overlay the simulated densities in light gray
for (i in 1:1) {
    lines(density(y_sim[[i]]), col = rgb(1, 0, 0, alpha = 1))
}

legend("topright",
    legend = c("Empirical KDE", "Model Simulations"),
    col = c("black", "red"), lwd = c(2, 1)
)
```

```{r}
# Create a grid of y values
y_grid <- seq(0, max(y), length.out = 200)

# Get predicted means (mu) and the estimated shape (dispersion)
mu_vals <- predict(model, type = "response")
# For Gamma GLM, shape = 1 / dispersion
shape_est <- 1 / summary(model)$dispersion

# Calculate the mean density across all observations for each point in the grid
marginal_dens <- sapply(y_grid, function(yi) {
    mean(dgamma(yi, shape = shape_est, scale = mu_vals / shape_est))
})
# empirical KDE data
emp <- density(y)
df_emp <- data.frame(x = emp$x, y = emp$y)

# analytical marginal data
df_ana <- data.frame(x = y_grid, y = marginal_dens)

# combined plot
p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +
    geom_line(data = df_emp, aes(x = x, y = y, color = "Empirical KDE"), linewidth = 1) +
    geom_line(
        data = df_ana, aes(x = x, y = y, color = "Analytical Marginal"),
        linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(
        "Empirical KDE" = "black",
        "Analytical Marginal" = "blue"
    )) +
    labs(
        title = "Empirical vs. Analytical Marginal Density",
        x = "y",
        y = "Density",
        color = NULL
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    scale_color_npg() + # Nature Publishing Group style
    theme(
        legend.position = "top",
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_text(color = "grey30"),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "grey40"),
        plot.title = element_text(color = "grey10"),
        legend.text = element_text(color = "grey10"),
        legend.title = element_text(color = "grey10"),
        legend.margin = margin(b = -5) # pull legend closer
    )
p
ggsave("./figures/ggplot_gamma_fit_gamma_data.svg", plot = p, width = 6, height = 4)
ggsave("./figures/ggplot_gamma_fit_gamma_data.pdf", plot = p, width = 6, height = 4)

pdf("./figures/gamma_fit_gamma_data.pdf", width = 6, height = 4)
ylim_range <- range(c(marginal_dens))
plot(density(y), ylim = ylim_range, lwd = 2, main = "Empirical vs. Analytical Marginal Density")
lines(y_grid, marginal_dens, col = "blue", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
legend("topright",
    legend = c("Empirical KDE", "Analytical Marginal"),
    col = c("black", "blue"), lty = c(1, 2), lwd = 2
)
dev.off()

model <- glm(y ~ x, family = gaussian())
# Get predicted means (mu) and the estimated shape (dispersion)
mu_vals <- predict(model, type = "response")

# For Gamma GLM, shape = 1 / dispersion
sigma_est <- 1 / summary(model)$dispersion

# Calculate the mean density across all observations for each point in the grid
marginal_dens <- sapply(y_grid, function(yi) {
    mean(dnorm(yi, mean = mu_vals, sd = sqrt(sigma_est)))
})

# empirical KDE data
emp <- density(y)
df_emp <- data.frame(x = emp$x, y = emp$y)

# analytical marginal data
df_ana <- data.frame(x = y_grid, y = marginal_dens)

# combined plot
p <- ggplot() +
    geom_line(data = df_emp, aes(x = x, y = y, color = "Empirical KDE"), linewidth = 1) +
    geom_line(
        data = df_ana, aes(x = x, y = y, color = "Analytical Marginal"),
        linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(
        "Empirical KDE" = "black",
        "Analytical Marginal" = "blue"
    )) +
    labs(
        title = "Empirical vs. Analytical Marginal Density",
        x = "y",
        y = "Density",
        color = NULL
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    scale_color_npg() + # Nature Publishing Group style
    theme(
        legend.position = "top",
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_text(color = "grey30"),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "grey40"),
        plot.title = element_text(color = "grey10"),
        legend.text = element_text(color = "grey10"),
        legend.title = element_text(color = "grey10"),
        legend.margin = margin(b = -5) # pull legend closer
    )
p
ggsave("./figures/ggplot_gaussian_fit_gamma_data.svg", plot = p, width = 6, height = 4)
ggsave("./figures/ggplot_gaussian_fit_gamma_data.pdf", plot = p, width = 6, height = 4)


pdf("./figures/gaussian_fit_gamma_data.pdf", width = 6, height = 4)
ylim_range <- range(c(marginal_dens))
plot(density(y), lwd = 2, main = "Empirical vs. Analytical Marginal Density")
lines(y_grid, marginal_dens, col = "blue", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
legend("topright",
    legend = c("Empirical KDE", "Analytical Marginal"),
    col = c("black", "blue"), lty = c(1, 2), lwd = 2
)
dev.off()
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

set.seed(123)

# Data (mixture distribution)
n <- 1500
# x <- c(rnorm(n / 3, -2, 1), rnorm(n / 3, 2, 0.5), runif(n / 3, -3, 3))
x <- c(rnorm(n / 3, -2, 1), rnorm(n / 3, 2, 0.5))

x_grid <- seq(-5, 5, length.out = 500)

true_density <- dnorm(x_grid, -2, 1) +
    dnorm(x_grid, 2, 0.5)

# true_density <- dnorm(x_grid, -2, 1) +
#     dnorm(x_grid, 2, 0.5) + dunif(x_grid, -3, 3)

binSize <- x_grid[2] - x_grid[1]
normalizer <- sum(binSize * true_density)
normalizer

true_density <- true_density / normalizer
sum(binSize * true_density)

bw_small <- 0.05
bw_large <- 3
bw_best <- bw.SJ(x)

dens_small <- density(x, bw = bw_small, from = -5, to = 5, n = 500)
dens_large <- density(x, bw = bw_large, from = -5, to = 5, n = 500)
dens_best <- density(x, bw = bw_best, from = -5, to = 5, n = 500)

# Helper to make a single plot
make_plot <- function(dens, title) {
    ggplot() +
        # geom_density(aes(x, fill = "Empirical"), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
        geom_histogram(
            aes(x = x, y = after_stat(density)),
            fill = "grey90",
            color = NA,
        ) +
        geom_line(aes(dens$x, dens$y, color = "Density estimator"), linewidth = 1) +
        geom_line(aes(x_grid, true_density, color = "True density"), linewidth = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
        labs(title = title, x = "x", y = "Density", color = NULL) +
        theme_bw() +
        scale_color_npg() + # Nature Publishing Group style
        theme(
            panel.grid = element_blank(),
            axis.text = element_text(color = "grey30"),
            axis.title = element_text(color = "grey40"),
            plot.title = element_text(color = "grey10"),
            legend.text = element_text(color = "grey10"),
            legend.title = element_text(color = "grey10"),
            legend.margin = margin(b = -5) # pull legend closer
        )
}

p1 <- make_plot(dens_small, paste0("Small bw = ", bw_small))
p2 <- make_plot(dens_best, paste0("Best bw = ", round(bw_best, 3)))
p3 <- make_plot(dens_large, paste0("Large bw = ", bw_large))

# Arrange in one row
p = p1 + p2 + p3 + plot_layout(ncol = 3, guides = "collect") &
    theme(legend.position = "top")
p
ggsave("./figures/ggplot_bw.svg", plot = p, width = 6, height = 4)
ggsave("./figures/ggplot_bw.pdf", plot = p, width = 6, height = 4)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

set.seed(123)

# -----------------------------
# 1. Simulate data (mixture)
# -----------------------------
n <- 500
x <- c(
    rnorm(n / 2, mean = -2, sd = 0.8),
    rnorm(n / 2, mean = 2, sd = 1.0)
)

df <- data.frame(x = x)

# -----------------------------
# 2. Grid for evaluation
# -----------------------------
grid <- seq(min(x) - 3, max(x) + 3, length.out = 500)

# True density (mixture)
true_density <- 0.5 * dnorm(grid, -2, 0.8) +
    0.5 * dnorm(grid, 2, 1.0)

true_df <- data.frame(x = grid, y = true_density)

# -----------------------------
# 3. KDE with different bandwidths
# -----------------------------
kde_small <- density(x, bw = 0.2, from = min(grid), to = max(grid))
kde_large <- density(x, bw = 1.2, from = min(grid), to = max(grid))

kde_df <- rbind(
    data.frame(x = kde_small$x, y = kde_small$y, type = "Small bandwidth"),
    data.frame(x = kde_large$x, y = kde_large$y, type = "Large bandwidth")
)

# -----------------------------
# 4. Plot
# -----------------------------
ggplot() +
    # true density
    geom_line(
        data = true_df,
        aes(x = x, y = y),
        color = "black", linewidth = 1.2
    ) +

    # KDEs
    geom_line(
        data = kde_df,
        aes(x = x, y = y, color = type),
        linewidth = 1
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(
        "Small bandwidth" = "blue",
        "Large bandwidth" = "red"
    )) +
    labs(
        title = "Effect of Bandwidth on Kernel Density Estimation",
        x = "x",
        y = "Density",
        color = "Estimator"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

set.seed(123)

# -----------------------------
# 1. Mixture parameters
# -----------------------------
n <- 600

pi1 <- 0.4
pi2 <- 0.4
pi3 <- 0.2

a <- -5
b <- 5

# -----------------------------
# 2. Sample from mixture
# -----------------------------
z <- sample(1:3, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = c(pi1, pi2, pi3))

x <- numeric(n)

x[z == 1] <- rnorm(sum(z == 1), mean = -2, sd = 0.8)
x[z == 2] <- rnorm(sum(z == 2), mean = 2, sd = 1.0)
x[z == 3] <- runif(sum(z == 3), min = a, max = b)

df <- data.frame(x = x)

# -----------------------------
# 3. Grid for densities
# -----------------------------
grid <- seq(min(x) - 2, max(x) + 2, length.out = 600)

# True density
true_density <- 0.4 * dnorm(grid, -2, 0.8) +
    0.4 * dnorm(grid, 2, 1.0) +
    0.2 * dunif(grid, a, b)

true_df <- data.frame(x = grid, y = true_density)

# -----------------------------
# 4. KDE with two bandwidths
# -----------------------------
kde_small <- density(x, bw = 0.25, from = min(grid), to = max(grid))
kde_large <- density(x, bw = 1.5, from = min(grid), to = max(grid))

kde_df <- rbind(
    data.frame(x = kde_small$x, y = kde_small$y, type = "Small bandwidth (0.25)"),
    data.frame(x = kde_large$x, y = kde_large$y, type = "Large bandwidth (1.5)")
)

# -----------------------------
# 5. Plot
# -----------------------------
ggplot() +
    geom_line(
        data = true_df,
        aes(x = x, y = y),
        color = "black", linewidth = 1.2
    ) +
    geom_line(
        data = kde_df,
        aes(x = x, y = y, color = type),
        linewidth = 1
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(
        "Small bandwidth (0.25)" = "blue",
        "Large bandwidth (1.5)" = "red"
    )) +
    labs(
        title = "KDE vs True Density: Gaussian Mixture + Uniform Component",
        x = "x",
        y = "Density",
        color = "Estimator"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```
